LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BY DEFTS & MOVING PARTIES JACK CASPER AND EDWARD MACKAY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING (MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION NON PAYMENT OF WAGES, 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION WAITING TIME PENALTY, 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT, 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT TREATED AS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PARTNERSHIP OR PROMOTER LIABILITY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION WRONGFUL TERMINATION GRANTED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, AND 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION DENIED) WITH THE EXCEPTION THAT THE 6TH, 7TH, AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE TREATED AS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, AND RULING IS ON CONDITION PLTF OBTAIN CONTINUANCE OF TRIAL DATE FOR AT LEAST 120 DAYS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Mark C. Peters, Esq. 362-4442 for pltf & opposing party; Melissa A. Wurster, Esq. 397-6000 for defts & moving parties Jack Casper and Edward MacKay.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling (MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION NON PAYMENT OF WAGES, 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION WAITING TIME PENALTY, 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT, 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT TREATED AS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PARTNERSHIP OR PROMOTER LIABILITY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION WRONGFUL TERMINATION GRANTED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, AND 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION DENIED) with the exception that the 6th, 7th, and 8th causes of action are treated as motion for judgment on the pleadings and granted with leave to amend, and ruling is on condition pltf obtain continuance of trial date for at least 120 days. Pltf has 10 days to amend; deft has 30 days to respond. (302/AJR/AA) Court Reporter Patricia M. Evezich, CSR #1193
